commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Without, including what is styled the second city; (4 Kings xxii. 14,) or he perfected the works begun by his father, or repaired the wall which the Assyrians had thrown down, though we read not of their taking the city. (Calmet)
Fish-gate.to Ophel, from the north-eastern corner, to the place opposite to the eastern gate of the temple, chap. xxvii. 3. (Menochius)
The fish-gate led towards Joppe, whence the city was supplied with fish. (St. Jerome, Trad.)
It was also called David's gate, because it stood near his city. By it, the Chaldeans afterwards rushed in, Sophonias i. 10. (Tirinus)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
